title: hebanthe occidentalis
intro: Hebanthe occidentalis is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America, particularly in the Amazon rainforest, and is also known as "peiaçu" or "peiaçu-do-norte" in the local language. It is a shrub-like plant with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties and health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Hebanthe occidentalis can be prepared as an infusion or decoction, and its herbal preparations have been used to treat conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
intro_study: According to "Pharmaceutical biology", Hebanthe occidentalis, being a related plant to Heliopsis sinaloensis, could be a source of bioactive compounds against chronic-degenerative diseases.
